rulings.Case Title: PHYLLIS BLAZER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 18, 2024, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's unopposed Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. COMMONSPIRIT HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 18, 2024, Line 11, 2- DEFENDANT RICHARD MOONEY, M.D. INC. DBA WOMEN'S HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATES'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's (RICHARD MOONEY, M.D. INC. dba WOMEN'S HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATES) motion to strike is granted and denied as follows: The motion is denied as to the punitive damages allegations and prayer for exemplary damages. Plaintiff pleads sufficient ultimate facts and ratification to support the allegations. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC") pars. 26(o) and (r), 29, and 68.) The motion, however, is granted as to paragraph 144 and the claim for punitives on the twelfth cause of action, which sounds in negligence, as conceded. The motion is granted as to the prayer for "all statutory damages and/or Penalties as described herein in the California Civil Code, the California Government Code, or the California Labor Code, or as otherwise provided by law." Plaintiff does not allege the Labor and Government Code claims against Mooney. As to cause of action four under the Civil Code, the requested relief is unavailable. The motion is also granted as to the prayer for attorney's fees. Plaintiff does not highlight a basis for that recovery based on the claims she asserts against Mooney. The motion is granted as to the prayer for declaratory relief. Plaintiff does not allege a claim to support the relief. Declaratory relief is designed to provide prospective relief and the claim is not necessary or proper. (See Snyder v. California Ins. Guarantee Assn. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1207 ["Declaratory relief operates prospectively, and not merely for the redress of past wrongs." (cleaned up)]; CCP 1061 [court discretion to deny declaratory relief].) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)
